body:
status=open
reason=Bash tool in this execution environment is failing with ENOSPC on every invocation ("no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/...'"). The harness cannot allocate its per-command working directory under /tmp because the host /tmp (or root) filesystem is full. This blocks every verification step required by this bead's AC (cd cli && go test ..., lefthook run pre-commit) and also blocks git add/commit. Read/Write/Edit tools still function (used to inspect claim_liveness.go and execution_resources.go and to write this file), but no command execution is possible. This is retryable once the host frees disk space (either via operator intervention or another process's cleanup) — resubmit the bead once `df` on the execution host confirms free capacity. Investigation notes for whoever retries: cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:56-64 (writeAtomicClaimFile) creates `*.tmp-*` atomic sidecars directly under os.TempDir()/ddx-claim-heartbeats/<hash>/; cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go:250-267 (ExecutionResourcePreflight.checkRoot / checkRoots) only probes TempRoot + EvidenceRoots for free bytes/inodes and delegates to CleanupRunner (NewExecutionCleanupManager) for reclamation, but that cleanup manager does not currently touch the claim-liveness namespace under os.TempDir(); a fix should add a stale-tmp sweep (age-gated, e.g. keep files newer than a few minutes since writeAtomicClaimFile renames immediately after write) for ddx-claim-heartbeats/**/*.tmp-* and surface reclaimed file/inode counts in ExecutionCleanupSummary / resource.preflight events.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes
rationale: status: open

This blocks every verification step required by this bead's AC (cd cli && go test ..., lefthook run pre-commit) and also blocks git add/commit. Read/Write/Edit tools still function (used to inspect claim_liveness.go and execution_resources.go and to write this file), but no command execution is possible. This is retryable once the host frees disk space (either via operator intervention or another process's cleanup) — resubmit the bead once `df` on the execution host confirms free capacity. Investigation notes for whoever retries: cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go:56-64 (writeAtomicClaimFile) creates `*.tmp-*` atomic sidecars directly under os.TempDir()/ddx-claim-heartbeats/<hash>/; cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go:250-267 (ExecutionResourcePreflight.checkRoot / checkRoots) only probes TempRoot + EvidenceRoots for free bytes/inodes and delegates to CleanupRunner (NewExecutionCleanupManager) for reclamation, but that cleanup manager does not currently touch the claim-liveness namespace under os.TempDir(); a fix should add a stale-tmp sweep (age-gated, e.g. keep files newer than a few minutes since writeAtomicClaimFile renames immediately after write) for ddx-claim-heartbeats/**/*.tmp-* and surface reclaimed file/inode counts in ExecutionCleanupSummary / resource.preflight events.
decision_audit={"failure_class":"no_changes","retry_action":"retry","escalation_count":1,"requested_route":{"harness":"claude","model":"sonnet","profile":"default","routing_intent_source":"cli"},"actual_route":{"harness":"claude","model":"sonnet","route_reason":"cli"},"review_status":"skipped","review_skip_reason":"review_not_configured","review_classification":"review_skipped","land_status":"not_landed","reconcile_status":"not_applicable"}
